title: Syzygium quadrisepalum
intro: Syzygium quadrisepalum, also known as the mountain apple or water apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, contributing to its potential health benefits in treating fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Syzygium quadrisepalum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and capsules, which are often used in traditional medicine to support immune function and alleviate various health conditions.
taxonomy: Syzygium quadrisepalum is a plant species within the family Myrtaceae. It belongs to the order Myrtales and the clade Rosids. This classification suggests that Syzygium quadrisepalum shares characteristics with other flowering plants in the Myrtaceae family. Further, its classification places it within the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Overall, the classification of Syzygium quadrisepalum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants.
